title: Creating Custom Specs
text: code_spec() creates new CodeSpec scratch. Provide short condition ID, label, named list code vectors: resulting spec object $get_codes() $get_defs() methods:
code: my_htn <- code_spec(   condition = "my_htn",   label     = "Hypertension (study version)",   version   = "v1",   codes     = list(     dx_icd9  = c("4010", "4011", "4019"),     dx_icd10 = c("I10")   ) )  my_htn #>  #> ── Hypertension (study version) (v1) ─────────────────────────────────────────── #> Condition: `my_htn` #> Code sets: #>   `dx_icd9`: 3 condition / 3 outcome codes #>   `dx_icd10`: 1 condition / 1 outcome codes my_htn$get_codes(code_type = "dx_icd10") #> $dx_icd10 #> [1] "I10"

title: Creating Custom Specs
text: drug_spec() creates DrugSpec vector generic names:
code: my_thiazide <- drug_spec(   drug_class    = "my_thiazide",   label         = "Thiazide Diuretics (study subset)",   version       = "v1",   generic_names = c("HYDROCHLOROTHIAZIDE", "CHLORTHALIDONE", "INDAPAMIDE") )  my_thiazide$get_generics() #> [1] "HYDROCHLOROTHIAZIDE" "CHLORTHALIDONE"      "INDAPAMIDE"

what: Adding codes to a bundled spec
title: Creating Custom Specs
text: add_codes() returns modified deep clone spec additional codes merged . ... argument names must valid code-set keys. original never modified: default, added codes flagged condition outcome. Use variable_type restrict membership:
code: # Inspect available keys names(spec_htn_v1$get_codes()) #> [1] "dx_icd9"  "dx_icd10"  # Add a code and confirm the clone grew; original is unchanged htn_plus <- add_codes(spec_htn_v1, dx_icd10 = c("I99"))  length(htn_plus$get_codes(code_type = "dx_icd10")$dx_icd10)  # clone #> [1] 24 length(spec_htn_v1$get_codes(code_type = "dx_icd10")$dx_icd10)  # original #> [1] 23 htn_outcome_only <- add_codes(   spec_htn_v1,   variable_type = "outcome",   dx_icd10      = c("I99") )

what: Removing codes from a bundled spec
title: Creating Custom Specs
text: remove_codes() returns clone specified codes excluded:
code: htn_trimmed <- remove_codes(spec_htn_v1, dx_icd9 = c("4019"))  # Code removed from clone; original is unchanged "4019" %in% htn_trimmed$get_codes(code_type = "dx_icd9")$dx_icd9 #> [1] FALSE "4019" %in% spec_htn_v1$get_codes(code_type = "dx_icd9")$dx_icd9 #> [1] TRUE

title: Creating Custom Specs
text: modify_code_spec() can update label, narrative definitions, replace code sets: create new version, construct new code_spec() object directly rather modifying existing one. keeps version explicit self-contained:
code: htn_revised <- modify_code_spec(   spec  = spec_htn_v1,   label = "Hypertension (revised)",   defs  = list(     condition = c("i" = "Study-specific hypertension definition."),     outcome   = NULL   ) )  htn_revised #>  #> ── Hypertension (revised) (v1) ───────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Condition: `htn` #> Condition def: #> ℹ Study-specific hypertension definition. #>  #> Code sets: #>   `dx_icd9`: 13 condition / 0 outcome codes #>   `dx_icd10`: 23 condition / 0 outcome codes spec_my_htn_v2 <- code_spec(   condition = "my_htn",   label     = "Hypertension (study v2)",   version   = "v2",   defs      = list(     condition = c("i" = "Expanded definition including I11.x and I13.x."),     outcome   = NULL   ),   codes = list(     dx_icd10 = c("I10", "I110", "I119", "I130", "I132")   ) )  spec_my_htn_v2 #>  #> ── Hypertension (study v2) (v2) ──────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Condition: `my_htn` #> Condition def: #> ℹ Expanded definition including I11.x and I13.x. #>  #> Code sets: #>   `dx_icd10`: 5 condition / 5 outcome codes

title: Creating Custom Specs
text: modify_drug_spec() mirrors modify_code_spec() drug classes. work specific component composite, extract first:
code: # Extract a leaf spec from a composite acei_v1 <- spec_antihypertensive$components()$acei_v1  # Build a study-specific variant my_acei <- modify_drug_spec(   spec          = acei_v1,   label         = "ACE Inhibitors (study subset)",   generic_names = c("LISINOPRIL", "RAMIPRIL", "ENALAPRIL") )  my_acei$get_generics() #> [1] "LISINOPRIL" "RAMIPRIL"   "ENALAPRIL"

title: Creating Custom Specs
text: custom-spec functions return deep clones. Bundled spec objects (spec_htn_v1, spec_antihypertensive, etc.) never modified place, can safely build study-specific variants without affecting code project relies standard definitions.

what: Composite specs and the component argument
title: Getting Started
text: specs composites — contain several named component specs rather single code set. spec_ascvd, example, groups coronary heart disease (CHD), stroke, lower extremity artery disease (LEAD) / peripheral arterial disease (PAD), cerebrovascular disease single composition. composite specs, component argument required. Print spec see available component names: Retrieve codes single component: Use component = "" get union every component’s codes:
code: spec_ascvd #>  #> ── Atherosclerotic Cardiovascular Disease (ASCVD) (composite) ────────────────── #> Condition: `ascvd` #> Def: Composition of all ASCVD component specs: coronary heart disease (CHD), #> stroke, lower extremity artery disease (LEAD) / peripheral arterial disease #> (PAD), and cerebrovascular disease #> Components: #>   `chd_v1`: Coronary Heart Disease #>   `chd_v2`: Coronary Heart Disease #>   `stroke_v1`: Stroke (Any) #>   `lead_pad_v1`: lower extremity artery disease (LEAD) / peripheral artery #>   disease (PAD) #>   `cerebrovasc_disease_v1`: Cerebrovascular Disease #> Use `component` = "chd_v1", "chd_v2", "stroke_v1", "lead_pad_v1", and #> "cerebrovasc_disease_v1" in `get_*()` functions. get_ascvd_codes(component = "chd_v1", code_type = "dx_icd10") #> $dx_icd10 #>  [1] "I21"    "I210"   "I2101"  "I2102"  "I2109"  "I211"   "I2111"  "I2119"  #>  [9] "I212"   "I2121"  "I2129"  "I213"   "I214"   "I219"   "I21A"   "I21A1"  #> [17] "I21A9"  "I22"    "I220"   "I221"   "I222"   "I228"   "I229"   "I2510"  #> [25] "I25810" "I25811" "I25812" "I253"   "I2541"  "I2542"  "Z951"   "Z9861"  #> [33] "I200"   "I201"   "I208"   "I209"   "I240"   "I241"   "I248"   "I252"   #> [41] "I255"   "I2582"  "I2583"  "I2584"  "I2589"  "I259" get_ascvd_codes(component = "all", code_type = "dx_icd10") |> length() #> [1] 1

title: ICD-10-PCS Code Expansion
text: peridefs bundles CMS FY2026 ICD-10-PCS code table (effective October 1, 2025), contains 79,115 valid codes. expand_pcs() validates expansions table — return codes appear official CMS list.

title: ICD-10-PCS Code Expansion
text: expand_pcs() works prefix matching: strips trailing x X characters pattern form fixed prefix, returns every valid code starts prefix. lowercase x uppercase X stripped trailing end, length trailing wildcard string matter — non-wildcard prefix used matching. pattern trailing x/X treated exact code lookup: exact code exist reference table, empty vector returned:
code: # "0210xxx" → prefix "0210" → all valid codes starting with 0210 cabg_pcs <- expand_pcs("0210xxx") length(cabg_pcs) #> [1] 74 head(cabg_pcs) #> [1] "0210083" "0210088" "0210089" "021008C" "021008F" "021008W" # Exact code — returned only if it exists in the FY2026 table expand_pcs("02100Z9") #> [1] "02100Z9" expand_pcs("0000000") #> character(0)

title: ICD-10-PCS Code Expansion
text: Pass character vector expand several patterns . Results union matches, deduplicated:
code: # CABG with two different approach prefixes combined <- expand_pcs(c("0210xxx", "0211xxx")) length(combined) #> [1] 148

what: How bundled PCS specs are built
title: ICD-10-PCS Code Expansion
text: Bundled specs include ICD-10-PCS codes — spec_chd_v1 (coronary revascularization procedures) — use expand_pcs() internally build process data-raw/build_specs.R. stored code vectors always reflect FY2026 reference table. CMS releases new fiscal year update (typically October 1), package maintainer runs data-raw/download_pcs_codes.R refresh reference table, rebuilds affected specs.
code: # ICD-10-PCS codes bundled in spec_chd_v1 spec_chd_v1 <- spec_ascvd$components()[['chd_v1']] spec_chd_v1$get_codes(code_type = "proc_icd10")$proc_icd10 |> length() #> [1] 575

text: Stores code sets narrative algorithm description single version medical condition definition. version distinct object (e.g., spec_htn_v1, spec_htn_v2). spec stores: defs: named list elements condition outcome, holding narrative algorithm description string (NULL defined). codes: named list key-level lists keyed {code_type}_{encounter} (e.g., dx_icd9). key-level list : codes: character vector exact short-format codes (periods). condition: logical vector parallel codes; TRUE code belongs condition/history definition. outcome: logical vector parallel codes; TRUE code belongs outcome definition. exclusions: optional character string describing codes exclude.

title: Create a user-defined condition code specification — code_spec
text: condition Short identifier string, e.g. "my_cond". label Human-readable label, e.g. "Condition". version Optional version label string, e.g. "v1". Stored metadata ; use distinct object names (e.g., my_spec_v1) distinguish versions. defs Named list elements condition /outcome, character string describing algorithm. NULL elements allowed. codes Named list key-level lists keyed {code_type}_{encounter} (e.g., "dx_icd9"). element may : character vector codes — condition outcome flags set TRUE codes. list elements codes, condition (logical vector), outcome (logical vector), optionally exclusions. exclusions Optional named list exclusion note strings, one per code-set key.

title: Expand ICD-10-PCS prefix patterns to all matching valid codes — expand_pcs
text: Resolves ICD-10-PCS pattern strings form used Perisphere definitions document (e.g., "0210xxx") full set valid 7-character ICD-10-PCS codes FY2026 CMS order file. Patterns matched prefix: trailing lowercase x characters stripped form prefix, valid codes begin prefix returned. Codes trailing x treated exact codes returned exist reference table.

text: code_type Optional character vector code types return. Valid values: "dx_icd9", "dx_icd10", "proc_icd9", "proc_icd10", "hcpcs", "cpt", "rev". NULL (default) returns code types. variable_type "condition" (default) "outcome". Hypertension defined condition ; "outcome" returns empty code sets. periods Logical. FALSE (default) returns short-format codes (e.g., "4010"). TRUE returns decimal-format codes (e.g., "401.0"). format "list" (default) returns named list character vectors. "tibble" returns long-form tibble columns code_type, code, variable_type. component Required. Component name, e.g. "chd_v1", "stroke_v1", "isch_stroke_v1", "hf_v1", "cerebrovasc_disease_v1".

what: peridefs: Perisphere Medical Code Definitions — peridefs-package
title: peridefs: Perisphere Medical Code Definitions — peridefs-package
text: Provides structured, versioned medical code definitions (ICD-9, ICD-10, HCPCS, CPT, Revenue codes, drug generic names) use claims-based research. Bundled code sets pre-expanded accessible consistent R6-based API covering medical conditions drug classes.

title: peridefs: Perisphere Medical Code Definitions — peridefs-package
text: CodeSpec: Versioned ICD/HCPCS/CPT code sets medical condition. DrugSpec: Versioned generic drug name (GNN) NDC sets drug class. CompositeCodeSpec: Union multiple CodeSpec objects (e.g., ASCVD). CompositeDrugSpec: Union multiple DrugSpec objects (e.g., antihypertensives).

title: peridefs: Perisphere Medical Code Definitions — peridefs-package
text: expand_pcs() function resolves prefix patterns (e.g., "0210xxx") matching valid ICD-10-PCS codes using bundled FY2026 CMS reference table.

title: peridefs: Perisphere Medical Code Definitions — peridefs-package
text: Maintainer: Perisphere info@perisphere-rwe.com

what: Condition and Drug Code Specification Objects — spec_objects
title: Condition and Drug Code Specification Objects — spec_objects
text: peridefs exports one spec_* object per medical condition version drug class. R6 object: Standalone conditions: spec single condition. specs versioned (e.g., spec_htn_v1, spec_htn_v2). Composite specs: collection standalone conditions single spec. example, atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease composition standalong specs coronary heart disease, stroke, cerebrovascular disease. Composite specs versioned multiple components versions. Use component= access individual components respective versions, component = "" get union components. Print spec see definition, code sets, (composites) available component names.
